title: Finding Your Address
description: Marek helps you give an address, explain apartment details, and guide someone to the right place.
context: Marek is coming to meet you and needs to find your building and flat. You give your address, describe the entrance and floor, and guide him with simple directions and position words.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Ahoj! Chcem prísť za tebou. Aká je tvoja adresa?
exchanges.translation: Hi! I want to come see you. What is your address?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks for your address. Pick a correct address answer.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Moja adresa je Hlavná ulica 12. — correct. This is the basic address pattern.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use Moja adresa je... when someone asks for your address.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.translation: Which city do you live in?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks for the city. What do you say?

choices.translation: I live on the ground floor.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Bývam v Bratislave. — correct. Bývam v + city is the useful city pattern.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: For city, use Bývam v + city.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.translation: Can you also send me the postal code?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks for the postal code. Pick the right field answer.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Moje PSČ je 811 01. — correct. PSČ is the postal-code field on Slovak forms.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: PSČ means postal code, so answer with Moje PSČ je...

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Som pri budove. Ktorý vchod je tvoj?
exchanges.translation: I am by the building. Which entrance is yours?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks for the entrance. What do you say?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Môj vchod je B. — correct. Vchod means entrance or staircase.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: For entrance, use Môj vchod je...

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: A ktoré poschodie? Mám ísť hore alebo dole?
exchanges.translation: And which floor? Should I go up or down?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks for the floor. Pick the apartment-detail answer.

choices.translation: I am on the second floor.
I live on the ground floor.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Correct. Poschodie, prízemie, and hore/dole help someone find the right floor.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use poschodie/prízemie or hore/dole when explaining floors.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks where the lift is. Pick the direction phrase.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Choďte priamo a potom vpravo. — correct. This combines straight ahead and right.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: For directions, use Choďte priamo, vpravo, or vľavo.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Som vo vnútri budovy. Kde si ty?
exchanges.translation: I am inside the building. Where are you?
exchanges.prompt: You are waiting outside. What do you say?
exchanges.wrongFeedback: If you are outside, say Som vonku.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Nevidím ťa. Si tu alebo tam?
exchanges.translation: I do not see you. Are you here or over there?
exchanges.prompt: Marek is nearby but looking the wrong way. Pick a here/there answer.
exchanges.correctFeedback: Tu, tam, and blízko are useful when pointing someone to a place.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use tu/tam/blízko when someone is trying to locate you.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.translation: What is next to your building?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks for a landmark. Pick the position phrase.

choices.translation: The pharmacy is next to the building.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Lekáreň je vedľa budovy. — correct. Vedľa means next to.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use vedľa when something is next to a place.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Som pri lekárni. Je tvoj vchod pred ňou alebo za ňou?
exchanges.translation: I am by the pharmacy. Is your entrance in front of it or behind it?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks for a precise position. Pick a valid position answer.

choices.translation: The entrance is in front of the pharmacy.
The entrance is behind the pharmacy.
The entrance is opposite the bank.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Pred, za, and naproti are precise position words for landmarks.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use pred, za, or naproti for precise landmark positions.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Už som skoro tam. Je byt nad obchodom alebo pod obchodom?
exchanges.translation: I am almost there. Is the flat above the shop or below the shop?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks about above/below. Pick the position phrase.

choices.translation: The flat is above the shop.
The flat is below the shop.

choices.text: Byt je medzi lekárňou a poštou.
choices.translation: The flat is between the pharmacy and the post office.

exchanges.correctFeedback: Nad, pod, and medzi give precise positions in and around buildings.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Use nad, pod, or medzi for above, below, and between.

exchanges.speaker: marek
exchanges.text: Našiel som to. Ďakujem! Nabudúce mi to môžeš napísať?
exchanges.translation: I found it. Thank you! Next time can you write it for me?
exchanges.prompt: Marek asks if you can write the address details next time. What is the useful phrase?
exchanges.correctFeedback: Napíšte to, prosím. — correct. It is useful for addresses and forms.
exchanges.wrongFeedback: Napíšte to, prosím means Write it down, please.

completionMessage: 🎉 Great job! You gave your address, explained your building and flat, followed directions, and used position words to help Marek find you.

Which city do you live in?
Can you also send me the postal code?

choices.label: Nad obchodom
choices.en: The flat is above the shop.

choices.label: Pod obchodom

choices.label: Medzi
choices.sk: Byt je medzi lekárňou a poštou.
choices.en: The flat is between the pharmacy and the post office.

questions.marekQuestionEn: I found it. Thank you! Next time can you write it for me?
